question: Can swarming a foreign repo about evolutionary algorithms improve swarm's own swarming?
expectation: OpenELM's MAP-Elites has structural analogs to swarm; reading will surface 1-2 missing mechanisms
actual: Found 3 critical gaps: (1) no selection pressure, (2) no QD score, (3) no niche competition. Built qd_score.py. First measurement: 51% of lessons are dominated.
diff: LARGE — expectation was 1-2 gaps, found 3 with quantitative evidence

structural_mapping.qd_score: sum of best Sharpe per niche (626 at N=1200)

falsification: If QD score doesn't correlate with swarm health (proxy-K, belief freshness) over 20 sessions, the MAP-Elites mapping is wrong
meta: First successful foreign-repo-to-swarm knowledge return (F-HLP6). The debrief protocol works.
